original: "Aye, that's right!" Caliban thumps his console. "That's why he's the old man! None of those flighty notions aristos and academicians love dreaming up."
translated: "Ouais, c'est ca !" Caliban frappe sa console du poing. "C'est pour ca qu'il est le Vieux ! Pas de ces idees fantasques que les aristos et les academiciens adorent inventer."

option_line: tseenKe_askDaudOpC2:"You don't think sacrifices are ever necessary?"
original: You don't think sacrifices are ever necessary?
translated: Vous ne pensez pas que des sacrifices sont parfois necessaires ?

original:
He sits back, puffs his cheeks out and blows air. As if to dispense with the heavy implications.

"That's above my pay-grade, captain. And I'm thankful for it." A grim look crosses his face. "If Daud asks it, I'll do it. That much I'll say."
translated:
Il se cale dans son siege, gonfle les joues et souffle. Comme pour evacuer les lourdes implications.

"C'est au-dessus de mon grade, capitaine. Et j'en suis reconnaissant." Un air sombre traverse son visage. "Si Daud le demande, je le ferai. Ca, je peux le dire."

original:
"Ha-ha! Could you now? I've heard that said often enough in the mess over one thing or another."

"Those are easy words when there's a logistics mixup or you're on the short end of command policy," Caliban says, taking on a philosophical tone. "But to be up there and have to make the call that's most right for all the millions? The higher you are, the more someone's always on the short end of your decision."
translated:
"Ha-ha ! Vraiment ? J'ai entendu ca assez souvent au mess pour une chose ou une autre."

"Ce sont des mots faciles quand il y a une erreur logistique ou qu'on est du mauvais cote d'une directive du commandement," dit Caliban, prenant un ton philosophe. "Mais etre la-haut et devoir prendre la decision la plus juste pour des millions de gens ? Plus on monte, plus quelqu'un se retrouve toujours du mauvais cote de votre decision."

original:
"Lot of stress, that. No right decision, sometimes. It makes enemies no matter what you do, sometimes." He shakes his head. "No, I'm best pleased where Command decides I should be. My problems are small and I can set 'em aside after a liter of beer and get a good sleep."

"Hear me, captain," he looks at you more seriously. "You don't want that."
translated:
"Beaucoup de stress, ca. Pas de bonne decision, parfois. Ca fait des ennemis quoi qu'on fasse, parfois." Il secoue la tete. "Non, je suis mieux la ou le Commandement decide que je dois etre. Mes problemes sont petits et je peux les oublier apres un litre de biere et une bonne nuit de sommeil."

"Ecoutez-moi, capitaine," il vous regarde plus serieusement. "Vous ne voulez pas de ca."

original:
"Ohhh, well now..." He rubs his chin. "He's a bit of a fancy lad, is Admiral Rao. Though not a bad one, not bad."

"Maybe when it's the old man's time to step aside," Caliban speaks with a shrug. "Rao wouldn't be so bad, maybe."
translated:
"Ohhh, eh bien..." Il se frotte le menton. "C'est un sacre dandy, l'amiral Rao. Mais pas un mauvais bougre, pas mauvais."

"Peut-etre quand il sera temps pour le Vieux de se retirer," dit Caliban avec un haussement d'epaules. "Rao ne serait pas si mal, peut-etre."

original:
"Hmm," he looks you over through the comm-link.

"That's nice. Go talk to someone who wants to deal with the bureaucracy to make it happen."
translated:
"Hmm," il vous examine par la liaison comm.

"C'est charmant. Allez parler a quelqu'un qui a envie de s'occuper de la bureaucratie pour que ca se fasse."

original:
He blows some air out through his nostrils, barely a laugh.

"Trading tired banter with you, it appears." He makes a show of checking a secondary display- for the time, perhaps.

"I am busy, if you can believe it. Perhaps you had something useful, or at least interesting to say?"
translated:
Il souffle de l'air par les narines, a peine un rire.

"A echanger des banalites usees avec vous, apparemment." Il fait mine de consulter un ecran secondaire - pour l'heure, peut-etre.

"Je suis occupe, si vous pouvez le croire. Peut-etre aviez-vous quelque chose d'utile, ou au moins d'interessant a dire ?"
